{"user_message": {"title": "ADR 0001 — Case-File Criteria Aggregation Defect Fixed (Meridian / Immigration Paperwork)", "content": "## ADR 0001 — Centralize O-1A criteria status aggregation

**Scope:** `src/features/case-file` · Status: Accepted · 2026-05-27

### Context
The highest-priority correctness defect in the reviewed area was in `CriteriaTable.tsx:10,23`:

```tsx
const met = criteria.filter((c) => c.status !== "Partial").length;
<Badge tone="success">{met} strong · 1 partial</Badge>
```

Three grounded problems:
1. **Hardcoded `1 partial`** — the partial count is a literal while qualifying is computed. Any change to `data.ts` (a 2nd `Partial`, a removed criterion) makes the eligibility badge *silently lie* — unacceptable on a legal/eligibility surface.
2. **Unsafe data-shape assumption** — `status !== "Partial"` counts *any* non-Partial value (a typo, or an absent status from upstream Gemini/Document AI scoring) as qualifying, inflating the count.
3. **Static `success` tone** regardless of whether the 3-criteria threshold is actually met.

`src/lib/format.ts` was also inspected and found sound for its current numeric inputs — no change needed there.

### Decision
Extracted a pure aggregator `summarizeCriteria(items)` → `{ total, qualifying, partial, meetsThreshold }` with explicit `QUALIFYING_THRESHOLD = 3` in new `src/features/case-file/criteria.ts`. It counts only exact `Met`/`Strong` as qualifying, only `Partial` as partial, and **ignores malformed rows** (non-array input, unknown/absent status) instead of miscounting. `CriteriaTable.tsx` now derives every count and the badge tone from it.

### Consequences
- Eligibility read-out stays truthful as data evolves; tone reflects the real threshold.
- **Render unchanged for current data** (7 qualifying · 1 partial · 8 evaluated, success tone) — verified.
- `tsc --noEmit` clean. Aggregation is now unit-testable & reusable.
- Established a lightweight `node:test` + `tsx` test convention (no test-runner dependency added).

## Task breakdown (shipped this run + follow-ups) — 5 tasks · 8 pts

| # | Task | Scope | Pts | Status |
|---|------|-------|-----|--------|
| 1 | Add `criteria.ts` aggregator | Pure `summarizeCriteria` + `QUALIFYING_THRESHOLD` | 2 | ✅ Done |
| 2 | Wire `CriteriaTable` to aggregator | Replace hardcoded counts + threshold-based tone | 1 | ✅ Done |
| 3 | Regression test `criteria.test.ts` | 4 cases incl. data-drift/malformed input (`tsx --test`) | 2 | ✅ Done (4/4 pass) |
| 4 | Publish ADR to `docs/adr/0001-…md` | Decision log entry | 1 | ✅ Done |
| 5 | Follow-up: drive `92% approval likelihood` (CaseFileDashboard.tsx) from real aggregation instead of literal | Same class of hardcoded-summary defect | 2 | ⏳ Proposed |

**Verification:** `npx tsx --test src/features/case-file/criteria.test.ts` → 4 pass, 0 fail. `npx tsc --noEmit` → clean. No existing rendering broken.

Files changed: `src/features/case-file/criteria.ts` (new), `criteria.test.ts` (new), `components/CriteriaTable.tsx` (edited), `docs/adr/0001-criteria-status-aggregation.md` (new).", "content_type": "success", "priority": "normal"}}

{"user_message": {"title": "Code Review — APPROVE (Case-File Criteria Aggregation Fix, ADR 0001)", "content": "## Code Review — Case-File Criteria Aggregation Fix

**Scope reviewed:** `src/features/case-file/criteria.ts` (new), `criteria.test.ts` (new), `components/CriteriaTable.tsx` (edited), plus `data.ts`, `types.ts`, adjacent `CaseFileDashboard.tsx` and `components/ui/Badge.tsx` for contract checks.

**Independent verification (re-run, not trusted from handoff):**
- `npx tsx --test src/features/case-file/criteria.test.ts` → **4/4 pass**
- `npx tsc --noEmit` → **clean (exit 0)**
- `Badge` `tone` accepts `neutral|accent|success|warning|danger` — every tone the table passes (`success`, `warning`) is valid. ✅
- Data sanity: `data.ts` has 6×Met + 1×Strong + 1×Partial → qualifying 7, partial 1, total 8 — matches the asserted no-regression render. ✅

The core fix is sound: the hardcoded `1 partial` is gone, every count and the badge tone now derive from the pure `summarizeCriteria` aggregator, malformed rows are excluded rather than miscounted, and the threshold (3) is explicit. This is a correct, well-tested resolution of a real eligibility-misreporting defect on a legal surface.

- **Fixed** — `CriteriaTable` no longer hardcodes `1 partial`; it now derives both qualifying and partial counts, so the badge stays accurate as case data changes.
- **Changed** — badge tone is now dynamic (`success` at/above threshold, `warning` below).

No public API break and no reinstall/migration required → not MINOR/MAJOR.

### State reconciliation
`package.json` was stuck at `0.1.0` while `CHANGELOG` already had a `[0.2.0]` entry (the Next.js 16 / React 19 / Tailwind 4 platform upgrade) that was **never finalized — no version bump, no tag**. This run:
- Finalized the version file to `0.2.1`.
- **Appended** a `[0.2.1]` changelog section above `[0.2.0]` (history preserved, nothing overwritten).
- Created annotated tag **`v0.2.1`** on the release commit, and **backfilled `v0.2.0`** on the framework-upgrade commit (`35486fd`) for a complete audit trail.

### Release notes (developer-oriented)
> **v0.2.1** — The O-1A criteria badge now tells the truth. Eligibility counts (qualifying / partial / meets-threshold) are computed by a new, unit-tested `summarizeCriteria()` helper instead of a hardcoded label, and the badge turns `warning` when a case falls below the 3-criterion threshold. Pure front-end logic change — no dependency or migration impact.

### Verification
- ✅ `tsc --noEmit` clean (exit 0).
- ⚠️ `node --test` could not execute `criteria.test.ts` directly — Node's type-stripping runner rejects the extensionless ESM imports (`./criteria`, `./types`). This is a **test-runner resolution quirk**, not a code defect; the same imports resolve under the Next.js/tsc bundler. Did not block the release (degrade-gracefully).
